title: Stress
lead: Stress is what happens when demands outweigh your sense of capacity. Some is normal and even useful — but when it never lets up, it takes a real toll.
explanation: Stress is your body and mind's response to pressure. Facing a challenge, your system releases hormones like cortisol and adrenaline to help you rise to it. Once the pressure passes, you're meant to return to calm.

The problem is chronic stress — pressure that never switches off. When the stress response stays on for weeks or months, it wears down sleep, mood, concentration, digestion, and the immune system, and can tip into anxiety, burnout, or depression.

You can't always remove the sources of stress, but you can change your relationship with it: recovering more fully between demands, protecting the basics, and asking for the support and changes you need.
symptoms: Feeling overwhelmed or unable to cope

immediate.title: Complete the stress cycle
immediate.description: Stress is physical, so it needs a physical release. A brisk walk, shaking out your body, or slow deep breaths signal to your nervous system that the threat has passed.

immediate.title: Brain-dump
steps: Write down everything on your mind, unfiltered
Circle only what you can act on today
Let the rest wait — it's captured, not lost

immediate.title: One-minute reset
steps: Stop and put both feet on the floor
Take three slow breaths with long exhales
Unclench your jaw and drop your shoulders
Ask: what's the one next thing that matters?

longTerm.title: Protect recovery
longTerm.description: Chronic stress is a recovery problem as much as a demand problem. Regular breaks, real rest, movement, and connection let your system reset instead of staying on high alert.

longTerm.title: Set boundaries
longTerm.description: Saying no, delegating, and lowering perfectionist standards aren't luxuries — they're how you keep stress within your capacity.

longTerm.title: Mindfulness-based stress reduction (MBSR)
longTerm.description: A well-researched programme that lowers stress by training present-moment awareness. Free versions are widely available.

resources.title: I'm so stressed out — fact sheet
resources.whyHere: Straightforward guide to stress vs. anxiety and what helps.

resources.title: Stress management
resources.whyHere: Practical, free NHS strategies for reducing everyday stress.

resources.title: UCLA Mindful
resources.whyHere: Free guided practices for stress, from a respected university programme.
